text.title: growth-social-media-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-social-media-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Choosing a platform because "everyone is on it" or because competitors are there without verifying ICP presence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competitors being on a platform does not mean your ICP converts there. Audience presence is the only criterion that matters at launch.

failure-mode.description: Spreading across too many platforms in the first 90 days.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dilutes content quality and engagement time before momentum builds on any single platform.

failure-mode.description: Giving up before 6 months of consistent posting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Audience momentum is non-linear. Most accounts see exponential growth between months 4-8, not in the first 30 days.

failure-mode.description: Using platform analytics only after paying for third-party tools.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Native platform analytics are free and sufficient for the first 6 months. Third-party tools add value only when cross-platform reporting becomes necessary.

failure-mode.description: Repurposing content across platforms verbatim (e.g., Twitter thread copy-pasted to LinkedIn).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each platform has distinct tone, format preferences, and algorithmic requirements. Verbatim cross-posting underperforms platform-native content by a significant margin.
